review_metadata.caveat: Codex was instructed not to re-read files in this pass; some assessments are directional only

target_journal_fit.jasss_verdict: In scope if paper narrows causal claims to mechanism exploration and adds discriminating tests
target_journal_fit.nhb_gap: Requires much stronger empirical identification, external validation, and generalizable behavioral contribution

theoretical_contribution.critique: Theoretical contribution may be framed stronger than model can support — must separate mechanism illustration from demonstrated explanation

structural_assumptions: Model conclusions may depend heavily on how roles/displacement are operationalized vs. independent empirical constraints
If agents discover roles because model architecture makes them payoff-relevant by construction, model embeds the result it claims to explain
Persistence of displacement is a strong claim unless retraining, institutional buffering, and endogenous role creation are represented
model_validity.is_virtual_role_finding_tautological: Potentially yes — if virtual roles emerge because state space already defines them as accessible and beneficial, finding is architectural not explanatory. Needs ablation analysis.
model_validity.displacement_persistence_issue: Not persuasive unless persistence survives alternative assumptions about adaptation speed, institutional response, and redistribution mechanisms

evidence_strength.historical_validation_verdict: Historical validation label only convincing with temporally disciplined out-of-sample tests, benchmark comparisons, and clear failure criteria
evidence_strength.what_real_validation_requires: Map model observables to archival series, pre-specify metrics, test competing mechanisms, reproduce multiple independent patterns, show robustness to alternative calibrations
overstatement_examples: Claiming model 'explains' historical change rather than 'reproduces selected patterns under specified assumptions'
Claiming emergent role adaptation validated by historical resemblance alone without discriminating tests
Claiming persistent displacement is likely real-world consequence without external anchoring

missing_literature: Foundational ABM validation literature (face validation vs. pattern-oriented modelling vs. external validation)
Equifinality and identification in simulation-based social science
Economic history of technological displacement, task creation, institutional mediation
Sociological work on role formation, occupational restructuring, welfare-state buffering
Causal inference limits in generative computational models

critical_issues.issue: Risk of presenting mechanism-generating ABM as validated evidence of real historical causation
critical_issues.fix: Reframe as exploratory mechanism analysis; add pre-specified external validation with discriminating tests

critical_issues.issue: Virtual role finding potentially tautological if model architecture makes those roles available by construction
critical_issues.fix: Add ablations, alternative state-space specs, negative controls

critical_issues.issue: Displacement persistence may be parameter-driven not robust mechanism
critical_issues.fix: Sensitivity over adaptation frictions, institutional response, endogenous task creation

critical_issues.issue: Evidential bridge from simulation to historical validation too weak
critical_issues.fix: Explicit empirical targets, out-of-sample tests, comparison against simpler baseline models

critical_issues.issue: Journal positioning may be mismatched if emphasizing broad behavioral claims without empirical grounding
critical_issues.fix: Position as methodological/theory-building contribution for computational social science

what_would_make_this_publishable_at_jass: Narrow claims to mechanism exploration, not historical explanation
Test findings against alternative representations of role discovery and adaptation
Distinguish calibration vs. pattern reproduction vs. external validation
Add benchmark comparisons against simpler models or null process models
what_would_make_this_publishable_at_nhb: Much stronger empirical identification linking model to observed behavioral/historical data
Model must outperform credible alternatives on multiple independent empirical targets
Clearer behavioral contribution generalizing beyond simulated case
Pre-registered validation criteria with serious out-of-sample testing
positive_aspects: Ambitious interdisciplinary framing relevant to computational social science
ABM is appropriate tool for exploring path dependence and role transitions IF claims stay proportionate
Methods appendix and historical validation protocol show commendable transparency
